\# MarketForge Action Execution Guide

\#\# Purpose

This guide explains how your business should understand and work with MarketForge actions after they have been approved.

MarketForge helps identify what to launch, package it for execution, and track what happens after launch.

\---

\#\# Execution stages

\#\#\# Approved  
The action has been reviewed and is ready to move into the launch queue.

\#\#\# Queued  
The action has been prepared for launch and key details have been entered.

\#\#\# Launched  
The action is live in market and should now be tracked for results.

\#\#\# Completed  
The action is no longer active and has been closed for reporting purposes.

\---

\#\# What your business should expect

For each launched action, MarketForge should help your business understand:

\- what service is being promoted  
\- what platforms are involved  
\- what offer and CTA are being used  
\- what the expected outcome range looks like  
\- how leads and booked jobs will be tracked

\---

\#\# What your business is responsible for

Your business should:

\- review actions before approval  
\- confirm service, offer, and contact information are correct  
\- provide access or launch details when needed  
\- report booked jobs and revenue clearly  
\- avoid guessing on attribution if the source is unclear

\---

\#\# What MarketForge is responsible for

MarketForge is responsible for:

\- packaging approved actions into launch-ready assets  
\- helping move actions through execution stages  
\- tracking lead and booked-job visibility where possible  
\- keeping launch and reporting workflows organized

\---

\#\# Best practices

\- Approve only actions that match real business priorities  
\- Make sure your business profile details stay accurate  
\- Report booked jobs consistently  
\- Use MarketForge reporting to understand which actions appear to be working

\---

\#\# Important note on attribution

Some channels are easier to attribute directly than others.

For example:

\- direct paid leads may be easier to link to specific actions  
\- SEO, AEO, or broader organic visibility may be harder to attribute with certainty

MarketForge aims for attribution that is honest, useful, and commercially meaningful.

\---

\#\# Summary

A good MarketForge workflow means:

\- the right actions get approved  
\- the right actions get launched  
\- stages are visible  
\- outcomes are tracked  
\- future recommendations improve over time
